# Scenario 1: Basic Transitive Resolution (What to Do When SBT Pulls in More Than You Declared)

## What This Scenario Demonstrates
This scenario demonstrates the fundamental concept of transitive dependency resolution in SBT.

## Dependency Graph
```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 4.4
graph LR
    Root[Project Root] --> LibA[lib-a:1.0.0]
    LibA --> LibB[lib-b:1.0.0]
    LibB --> LibC[lib-c:1.0.0]
```

### SBT (build.sbt)

```{.scala include="/demos/sbt-demo/scenario-1-basic/build.sbt" snippet="s1-deps"}
```

## Expected Intuition
All present.

## Actual Resolution Results
*   **SBT**: Resolves all.

## Classpath Reality
All present.

## Why This Happens
Standard resolution.

## How Developers Commonly "Fix" This
N/A

## Safer Ways to Take Control
N/A

## Signals to Watch For
N/A

## How This Scales in Real Systems
N/A

## Key Takeaway
SBT resolves transitively.

## Related Scenarios
*   [Scenario 2: Conflict](../scenario-2-conflict/guide.md)
